steps.title: Support prolonged water
steps.content: Moreno and Hale concluded that the wet and warm scenario is likely correct. That scenario requires prolonged warm, wet conditions on Mars, not a brief melting episode in an otherwise frozen environment.

steps.title: Reject short or ambiguous evidence
steps.content: A concerns whether the basin came from an impact, C supports a short-lived influx of water, and D is ambiguous because the channels could come from water or lava. Evidence of an ocean with gradual sea-level changes over an extended time directly supports sustained liquid water and prolonged wet conditions. The answer is B.
